reason:
Set 093 Session 2 — operator-attested close over an adjudicated-minor residual.

Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-6, anthropic excluded) ran 11 rounds. Every
material Critical/Major finding was fixed in-flight and re-verified, each with a
regression test: the module-action protocol fail-open, the stamp writer's
fence/placement/parse guards, the Open Plan Command-Palette mirror, and a
converging TOCTOU/atomicity series on the spec.md writer that was resolved
STRUCTURALLY by making the write atomic (temp -> verify -> rename, with a
pre-rename target re-check and a unique per-operation staging path).

Round 11's residual — serializing assignments across SEPARATE VS Code instances
via OS-level file-locking — is adjudicated-minor: it is unwinnable in portable
Node fs (no atomic conditional-replace primitive), disproportionate for stamping
a single `module:` line into a legacy set, and its practical probability (two
instances stamping the same unassigned set to different modules in the same
microsecond) is ~nil. Per the Set 086 severity-gated-stop rule, the operator
adjudicated "Accept & close" via AskUserQuestion.

Suite green at close: pytest 2922 passed / 6 skipped; extension unit 1467
passing; Playwright Layer 3 24 passed; tsc clean; live dogfood 17/17. The raw
per-round artifacts (s2-verification*.md, s2-issues*.json) are immutable records
of the full loop.
